caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 541618 is a residual code; no actual dominant revenue subsegment is established.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied labor share is anomalous and should not be read as literal economics.
inputs.pi_dist: Vendor pricing page demonstrates possible mechanisms, not 541618 revenue mix.
inputs.pi_moat: No code-level evidence measures proprietary data, switching costs or client retention.
inputs.t50_years: Both anchors are broad proxies and use different populations/question wording.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not a 541618 measurement; four-or-fewer firms are not identical to the target band.
inputs.historical_analogs: Evidence is a provider description and may not represent the code.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not a 541618, 60-plus or target-size observation.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: National evidence includes nonemployers and is not a specialty-consulting survey.
inputs.active_consolidators: A code-level count is not established.
inputs.buy_mult: S7 uses cash flow/SDE, not EBITDA; neither source is a 541618 closed-deal set, and owner compensation must be normalized.
inputs.exit_mult: No exit premium exists without verified contract recurrence and transferable client relationships.
inputs.pricing_structure: Dominant revenue segment is a hypothesis and must be identified before underwriting capture.
Validator: TEM is an expressly disclosed working subsegment, not a demonstrated dominant revenue cluster for residual NAICS 541618.
Validator: The 1.3991 deterministic labor share is economically anomalous and caps V at 10; use V only as a sensitivity until the QCEW/SUSB mismatch is reconciled.
Validator: Gallup documents national owner aging and planning gaps, not a 541618 successor shortage; the true shortage flag should not be treated as code-specific evidence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $247,727 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541618); class <5: 11,518 firms, avg revenue/firm $353,982 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $55,398 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 661 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,508,079 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $236,014 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 283 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,159,795 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $494,508 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 216 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,167,644 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,434,736 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 40 firms, avg revenue/firm $28,072,600 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,393,362 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $81,481,750 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,751,894 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 256

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Analytical, invoice and reporting workflows are AI-exposed, but code composition and the supplied 4-digit occupational proxy are highly uncertain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541618 ($14,897,754,779) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541618 ($11,352,826,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. The residual-code screen remains useful because its caution and proxy limitations are explicit, but TEM representativeness, the anomalous labor share, and the national succession proxy must remain visible publication caveats.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official 541618 residual-code definition, exclusions and explicit inclusion of telecommunications and utilities management consulting.

sources.title: O*NET OnLine: Management Analysts
sources.what_it_supports: Management-analyst tasks spanning analytical/reporting work as well as interviews, consultation and interpersonal activities.

sources.title: Monitoring AI Adoption in the US Economy
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS-based professional-services AI adoption of about 33% and the difference between adoption measures.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users
sources.what_it_supports: Census BTOS evidence that less than 20% of firms with four or fewer employees reported AI use and the 2025 question scope.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry: 2026 Data
sources.what_it_supports: Q1 2026 professional-services lower-middle-market EV/adjusted-EBITDA ranges and valuation drivers by EBITDA size.

sources.title: Understanding the Pricing Structure of TEM Solutions
sources.what_it_supports: TEM pricing models including 20-50% contingency fees on savings, fixed-hourly, fixed-license and fixed-scope fees.

sources.title: Business Acquisitions Stabilize as Buyers Get Selective
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 service-business sale activity and average 2.52 cash-flow multiple; this is a micro-service-market comparator, not an EBITDA or 541618 multiple.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: National owner-age and transition-planning evidence used only as a disclosed proxy for small-business succession conditions.

title: Other Management Consulting Services
